title: gaultheria suborbicularis
intro: Gaultheria suborbicularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, native to the high-altitude grasslands and rocky slopes of the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Gaultheria suborbicularis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Gaultheria suborbicularis can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
